attempts.error:
Predicate argument has wrong type:
  atLocation knife1 table1: knife1 is not a agent
  atLocation fridge1 table1: fridge1 is not a agent
  atLocation tomato1 table1: tomato1 is not a agent
attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: find a tomato to put on a table
    

SCENE CAPTION:
Scene Description:

The scene depicts a kitchen environment with a central grey table and a white refrigerator to the right. The floor is composed of large, square, beige tiles.

On the table, the following objects are present:
- A large, oval-shaped, brown loaf of bread, positioned near the center.
- A small, round, red tomato, located to the right of the bread.
- A black-handled knife with a dark grey blade, lying on the table to the right of the tomato.
- A black cylindrical cup, situated at the top edge of the table.
- A grey rectangular box or container, partially visible on the left side of the table.
- A grey rectangular object, possibly a cutting board or another container, positioned behind the cup.

The refrigerator is a white, two-door model with dark grey handles. The top door is closed. The refrigerator is positioned to the right of the table and is adjacent to a dark-colored cabinet or counter on its left side.

Relevant Object Types and States:
- The tomato is an object (not a receptacle) and is currently on the table.
- The knife is an object (specifically a knife type) and is on the table.
- The refrigerator is a receptacle and is currently closed (not opened). It is openable.
- The table itself is not defined as a receptacle in the domain, so objects on it are considered to be at their location, not in a receptacle.
- The microwave and sink are not visible in the images and are not present in the scene.

Spatial Relationships:
- The tomato is on the table, to the right of the bread and to the left of the knife.
- The knife is on the table, to the right of the tomato.
- The refrigerator is to the right of the table.
- The agent is not currently at any object’s location.

Current State:
- No objects are being held by the agent.
- The refrigerator is closed.
- The tomato is not sliced, not cleaned, not heated, and not cooled.
- The knife is not being held.
- No objects are in any receptacle (since the refrigerator is closed and the table is not a receptacle).

The goal is to find a tomato to put on a table. Since a tomato is already on the table, the task is already satisfied in the current state. However, if the instruction implies finding a tomato that is not currently on the table (e.g., inside the refrigerator), then the agent would need to open the refrigerator, retrieve a tomato (if present), and place it on the table. But based on the visible scene, there is no tomato inside the refrigerator, and the only tomato is already on the table.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem find_tomato_on_table)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        tomato1 - object
        knife1 - knife
        fridge1 - fridge
        table1 - object  ; table is not a receptacle per domain, so treated as object for location
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation agent1 table1)  ; agent is at the table to start, as it needs to interact with objects there
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
        (openable fridge1)
        (not (opened fridge1))
        (isOn table1)  ; table is considered "on" as a location
        (isSliced tomato1)  ; assuming tomato is already sliced if needed, but not required for goal
        (not (isClean tomato1))
        (not (isHot tomato1))
        (not (isCool tomato1))
        ; Tomato is on the table, so agent can interact with it directly
        (atLocation tomato1 table1)
        (atLocation knife1 table1)
        (atLocation fridge1 table1)  ; fridge is adjacent to table, so agent can go to it
    )
    (:goal (and
        (atLocation agent1 table1)
        (atLocation tomato1 table1)
    ))
)
```
attempts.problem:
(define (problem find_tomato_on_table)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        tomato1 - object
        knife1 - knife
        fridge1 - fridge
        table1 - object  ; table is not a receptacle per domain, so treated as object for location
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation agent1 table1)  ; agent is at the table to start, as it needs to interact with objects there
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
        (openable fridge1)
        (not (opened fridge1))
        (isOn table1)  ; table is considered "on" as a location
        (isSliced tomato1)  ; assuming tomato is already sliced if needed, but not required for goal
        (not (isClean tomato1))
        (not (isHot tomato1))
        (not (isCool tomato1))
        ; Tomato is on the table, so agent can interact with it directly
        (atLocation tomato1 table1)
        (atLocation knife1 table1)
        (atLocation fridge1 table1)  ; fridge is adjacent to table, so agent can go to it
    )
    (:goal (and
        (atLocation agent1 table1)
        (atLocation tomato1 table1)
    ))
)

- The table surface is smooth and gray, contrasting with the tiled floor.

Note: The instruction “find a tomato to put on a table” is already satisfied in the current scene, as the tomato is already on the table. (define (problem find_tomato_on_table)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        table - receptacle
        fridge - receptacle
        microwave - receptacle
        tomato - object
        black_cylinder - object
        grey_rectangle - object
        spatula - object
        bread_loaf - object
    )
    (:init
        (openable fridge)
        (openable microwave)
        (not (opened fridge))
        (not (opened microwave))
        (not (isOn microwave))
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
        (inReceptacle tomato table)
        (inReceptacle black_cylinder table)
        (inReceptacle grey_rectangle table)
        (inReceptacle spatula table)
        (inReceptacle bread_loaf table)
        (not (isClean tomato))
        (not (isHot tomato))
        (not (isCool tomato))
        (not (isSliced tomato))
    )
    (:goal (and (inReceptacle tomato table)))
)

final.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
